name: Casablanca
summary: Casablanca is Morocco's biggest city, sitting on the Atlantic coast where waves crash against bright white buildings. Its name means 'White House' in Spanish, and a giant mosque rises right out of the sea.
funFacts: The Hassan II Mosque sits partly over the ocean, and its 210-metre minaret shoots a green laser beam that points all the way toward Mecca at night.
The name 'Casablanca' is Spanish for 'White House', because so many of the city's buildings are painted bright white in the sunshine.
Moroccans pour sweet mint tea from really high up to make a frothy foam on top, and the higher the pour, the more respect for the guest.
White storks build huge stick nests on Moroccan rooftops and walls, and Moroccan stories say they are people who have magically turned into birds.
The famous old movie 'Casablanca' was actually filmed in Hollywood, but a fan later built a real Rick's Cafe in the city to match the film.
thingsToDo: Tour the giant Hassan II Mosque and walk on the plaza right above the crashing Atlantic waves.
Wander the narrow alleys of the Old Medina to spot pottery, leather slippers and sweet pastries.
Stroll the Corniche promenade at Ain Diab and watch surfers riding the Atlantic waves.
Step inside Rick's Cafe, a 1930s mansion built to look just like the bar from the famous old movie.
Peek through the doors of the Mahkama du Pacha, an old palace with carved wood and pretty tiles.
Spot white storks nesting on the towers of the Sacré-Cœur, an old white cathedral with pointy spires.

wildlife.name: Barbary Macaque
wildlife.description: Cheeky tail-less monkey with a fluffy face, found in Morocco's mountain forests.

wildlife.name: Dromedary Camel
wildlife.description: Tall desert animal with one big hump that stores fat for long journeys.

wildlife.name: White Stork
wildlife.description: Tall white bird with long red legs that builds huge nests on rooftops.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Bottlenose Dolphin
wildlife.description: Friendly grey dolphin that leaps from the waves off Morocco's coast.

localWord.meaning: Hello, or peace, used everywhere in Morocco.

images.alt: The huge Hassan II Mosque with its tall minaret rising beside the sparkling Atlantic Ocean in Casablanca.
A busy little street inside the Old Medina of Casablanca, full of shops and people.
The grand white walls and arched doorways of the Mahkama du Pacha palace in Casablanca.
The Casablanca Corniche promenade at Ain Diab lit up beside the Atlantic Ocean.
The white archway entrance to Rick's Café Casablanca, built to look like the famous movie bar.
The white spires of the Sacré-Cœur Cathedral rising into the Casablanca sky.
A Barbary macaque sitting on a branch in a Moroccan forest.
A dromedary camel with a single hump standing in Morocco.
A white stork with long red legs standing in its big stick nest.
An Atlantic bottlenose dolphin leaping out of the ocean waves.
